details.text: Derivative: دُخَانٍ refers to smoke and here it can refer to a smoke that appears on the Day of Judgement or to the appearance of smoke that forms in the sight of those who are suffering from hunger in a drought or famine.

Derivative: ارْتَقِبْ is the command form from اِرْتَقَبَ – اِرْتِقابٌ is to wait in anticipation of something happening which is usually something bad. To be watchful, look out for in anticipation or to lie in wait.
Derivative: مُعَلَّمٌ means to have been taught and implies that it is not original i.e., speech or ideas that originate from another person or source.
Derivative: مَّجْنُونٌ from جّنَّ means to be insane or to be possessed by a Jinn.
Derivative: أَدُّوٓا۟ means سَلِّموا i.e., to surrender, deliver and hand over.

Derivative: تَعْلُوا۟ from عَلَا which means to be high and elevated. When عَلى (upon or against) follows it, it means to act high and mighty or haughty with insolence and arrogance against something or someone.
Derivative: وَ – واوُ القَسْم (The Waw of the oath) is used in Arabic to draw your attention to a matter and to prepare you for a point that is about to be made.
Derivative: أَسْرِ from أَسْرَى which means to travel by night.
Derivative: رَهْوًا means stillness and unmoving. Here it means to leave the sea as it is i.e., in its current parted state.

Derivative: أَنزَلْنَا from أَنْزَلَ – إِنْزال which means to be revealed or to descend and implies that something descends or is revealed immediately in contrast to نَزَّلَ – تَنْزِيل which implies being revealed or descending over a period of time.

Derivative: مُّبَارَكَةٍ means blessed i.e., full of بَرَكَة (Barakah – Blessing). بَرَكَة (Barakah - Blessing) has two qualities attached to it: Firstly, that it produces more goodness than is anticipated and secondly, that it brings out the potential goodness in something.
Derivative: مُنشَرِينَ from أَنْشَرَ and means to be raised and resurrected.
Derivative: يُفْرَقُ from فَرَقَ – فَرْقٌ and here it means to separate, designate or specify.
Derivative: ٱلْأَثِيمِ is the one who is constantly immersed or engaged in sinful conduct.
